chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1559 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For katcha peshi. 3. For orders on MA-6139/2017 25.09.2017 Mrs. Razia Ali Zaman Patoli, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Ghulam Abbas Sangi, Assistant A.G a/w Inspector Kanwar Singh SHO P.S Town Mirpurkhas. = With reference to the notice of this case, Mr. Muhammad Asif Zai, Advocate files power alongwith objections on behalf of the respondent No.5, which are taken on record and copy of objections has been supplied to learned Counsel for the petitioner. Whereas, the learned Assistant A.G files parawise comments on behalf of the respondents No.2, 3 and 4, which are also taken on record and copies whereof have been supplied to the petitioner’s Counsel. It is stated in the parawise comments of the official respondents that the petitioner is a notorious criminal / land grabber / murderer and he has been challaned in many criminal cases. It also reflects from the parawise comments that the petitioner and the private respondent have filed criminal cases against each other, which are pending before the respective competent Courts of Law. Keeping in view the above scenario, the parties are directed to restrain themselves from taking law in their hands, whereas, the official respondents are directed to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. Since the parties have grievances against each other, therefore, they may seek remedy of their grievances by invoking due process of law but not by taking law in their hands.

Keeping in view the above scenario, the parties are directed to restrain themselves from taking law in their hands, whereas, the official respondents are directed to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. Since the parties have grievances against each other, therefore, they may seek remedy of their grievances by invoking due process of law but not by taking law in their hands. Petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE Shahid
